ALL SPECIALTIES / DIETITIAN / ALASKA

Photo: Unsplash / Unsplash

Alaska Dietitian License

Alaska Dietitian license requirements, fees, and steps.

Last updated 2026-02-25 · Sources verified

  • New Applicant
  • Reciprocity
  • Renewal

New Applicant

Dietitian applications in Alaska run through Dietitians and Nutritionists Program. Use the checklist below before starting your application.

Required documents

  • Official Application & Photo - Application with notarized signature and a recent passport-style photograph.
  • CDR Registration Verification - Must be sent directly from the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
  • Verification of Out-of-State Licenses - If applying with existing licenses, verification from all other states must be submitted.

Key timeline factors

  • Board processing 6 to 8 Weeks
  • Fingerprinting Required - complete this early to avoid delays.
  • Application completeness Missing or mismatched documents are the most common reason timelines extend.

Reciprocity

Alaska offers licensure by endorsement for those already licensed in another state. Applicants must demonstrate that their current state's requirements are substantially equivalent to Alaska's and must submit verifications for all current and past licenses.

  1. STEP 1

    Create MyLicense Account

    Visit the MyLicense portal (https://www.commerce.alaska.gov/MyLicense) to register for a new account or log in with your MyAlaska credentials. This is the primary system for all professional licensing applications in Alaska.

    Fee: $150 · Timeline: 6 to 8 Weeks

  2. STEP 2

    Complete Online Application

    Within the MyLicense portal, select the Board of Dietitians and Nutritionists and fill out the initial application form, providing all required personal, educational, and supervised practice experience details.

    Fee: $150 · Timeline: 6 to 8 Weeks

  3. STEP 3

    Submit Supporting Documentation

    Upload or arrange for official submission of all required documents, including official academic transcripts (e.g., bachelor's degree or higher in dietetics/nutrition), verification of 900 hours of supervised practice from an ACEND-accredited program, and proof of passing a national dietetic examination (e.g., CDR exam).

    Fee: $150 · Timeline: 6 to 8 Weeks

  4. STEP 4

    Pay Application and Initial License Fees

    Pay the non-refundable application fee ($50) and the prorated initial biennial license fee ($200 for a full cycle) through the MyLicense portal using a credit card.

    Fee: $150 · Timeline: 6 to 8 Weeks

  5. STEP 5

    Await Board Review and Approval

    The Alaska Board of Dietitians and Nutritionists will review your completed application and all supporting documents. Once approved, your license will be issued and accessible through the MyLicense portal.

    Fee: $150 · Timeline: 6 to 8 Weeks

Fees and Costs

Application fee
$100
Background check
$0 (included in application)
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$150

Always confirm current fees on the board site before submitting.

Board Contact

Board
Dietitians and Nutritionists Program
Phone
907-465-2580
Website
commerce.alaska.gov/web/cbpl/ProfessionalLicensing/DietitiansNutritionists.aspx
Application page
Open application

Renewal

Dietitian renewal in Alaska follows the board cycle below.

Renewal cycle
Every 2 years (expires Dec 31 of odd-numbered years)
Renewal fee
$125
Continuing education
30 hours every 2 years

Frequently Asked Questions

How much does an Alaska dietitian license cost?

The total initial cost is $150, which includes a $100 application fee and a $50 initial license fee.

When does the Alaska license need to be renewed?

Alaska dietitian licenses renew biennially and expire on December 31st of odd-numbered years. The renewal period requires a $125 fee. There is no grace period for practicing on a lapsed license.

Does Alaska require a background check?

While an extensive FBI fingerprint check is not centrally required for all applicants in this program, individuals must answer professional fitness questions and may need to provide court documents if they have a disciplinary history.

Does Alaska have reciprocity for dietitians?

No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Alaska. To work in Alaska, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Alaska?

Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

Can I start working in Alaska while my endorsement application is processing?

Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.

At a glance

Endorsement fee

$150

Processing time

6 to 8 Weeks

Fingerprinting

Required

Jurisprudence exam

Not required

Temporary license

Not available

National exam

Registration Examination for Dietitians

Compact

No · plan on endorsement

Renewal

Every 2 years (expires Dec 31 of odd-numbered years) · $125

Licensing board

Dietitians and Nutritionists Program

commerce.alaska.gov/web/cbpl/ProfessionalLicensing/DietitiansNutritionists.aspx

907-465-2580
